requirements.desc: R1: backtick at value start treated as opening double-quote
requirements.note: parseValue now accepts '`' and routes to parseString, which always emits '"' as the opening quote.

requirements.desc: R2: backtick or double-quote followed by structural delimiter ends value
requirements.note: String close detection now accepts '"' or '`' (for backtick-opened values), checks next non-space char for structural delimiter/EOF, and emits '"' on close.

requirements.desc: R3: backticks inside value body preserved
requirements.note: Non-terminating backticks in backtick-opened strings are emitted as normal content; added test asserts inner `code` is preserved.
Code changes appear backward-safe for normal JSON and existing tests were not modified, but the diff alone does not prove the full pre-existing unit suite was executed/passed.

requirements.desc: R5: open and close backtick value both substituted
requirements.note: Backtick-open/backtick-close normalization is implemented and covered by added tests that parse successfully.

unplanned.change: New draft specification document added (process/documentation artifact, not a functional requirement).
Flow lifecycle/metrics metadata added, outside runtime behavior requirements.
Gate evaluation log added, not part of parser behavior requirements.
Q&A template/confirmation file added, not tied to runtime requirements.
Full feature spec file added; documentation change not required by R1-R5 implementation behavior.
Test documentation README added; not a direct requirement item.

summary.notes: Core parser behavior for backtick-opened values (R1, R2, R3, R5) is implemented and test-covered in diff. R4 is only partially evidenced because full regression pass status is not directly proven by the shown diff. Several spec/process files were added beyond the behavior requirements.
